heading: International Space Station completion
label: 70904 International Space Station completion

children.heading: .—Policy
children.label: a .—Policy
children.text: It is the policy of the United States to achieve diverse and growing utilization of, and benefits from, the International Space Station.
be assembled and operated in a manner that fulfills international partner agreements, as long as the Administrator determines that the shuttle can safely enable the United States to do so;
be used for a diverse range of microgravity research, including fundamental, applied, and commercial research, consistent with ;section 40904 of this title

have an ability to support a crew size of at least 6 persons, unless the Administrator transmits to the Committee on Science and Technology of the House of Representatives and the Committee on Commerce, Science, and Transportation of the Senate not later than 60 days after , a report explaining why such a requirement should not be met, the impact of not meeting the requirement on the International Space Station research agenda and operations and international partner agreements, and what additional funding or other steps would be required to have an ability to support a crew size of at least 6 persons;December 30, 2005
support Crew Exploration Vehicle docking and automated docking of cargo vehicles or modules launched by either heavy-lift or commercially-developed launch vehicles;

support any diagnostic human research, on-orbit characterization of molecular crystal growth, cellular research, and other research that the Administration believes is necessary to conduct, but for which the Administration lacks the capacity to return the materials that need to be analyzed to Earth; and
be operated at an appropriate risk level.

children.heading: .—Policy
children.label: 1 .—Policy
children.text: The Administrator shall ensure that the International Space Station can have available, if needed, sufficient logistics and on-orbit capabilities to support any potential period during which the space shuttle or its follow-on crew and cargo systems are unavailable, and can have available, if needed, sufficient surge delivery capability or prepositioning of spares and other supplies needed to accommodate any such hiatus.

children.heading: .—Plan
children.label: 2 .—Plan
children.text: Before making any change in the International Space Station assembly sequence in effect on , the Administrator shall transmit to the Committee on Science and Technology of the House of Representatives and the Committee on Commerce, Science, and Transportation of the Senate a plan to carry out the policy described in paragraph (1).December 30, 2005
